# UC-22.60.1 · DO-326A Plan for Security Aspects of Certification (PSecAA) register and milestone tracker for type certifications and major modifications, with FAA / EASA finding-of-non-compliance early-warning

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Governance &middot; **Wave:** Crawl &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Before a new aircraft can fly with passengers, the regulator must sign off on its cyber-security plan. We watch every project that is going through this approval and make sure each step is on track, so the aircraft isn't delayed.*

---

## Description

Reconciles every active type-certification (TC) and major-modification (STC) project against its DO-326A PSecAA acceptance, then tracks all downstream milestones (CSI register baseline, SRA completion, security architecture freeze, security verification, CASI delivery) against their certification-authority-agreed due dates. Surfaces overdue and at-risk milestones to the Chief Engineer and the CISO before they become a formal finding of non-compliance.

## Value

A finding of non-compliance with the PSecAA at FAA or EASA finalisation can delay type-certificate (TC) issuance by 3-12 months, with knock-on revenue loss measured in hundreds of millions of dollars per programme. The Boeing 737 MAX cyber recertification and the Airbus A350 ANSP project both demonstrated that DO-326A artefacts are on the critical path for first-flight and entry-into-service. This UC closes the gap before the certification authority sees it.

## Implementation

(1) Populate `do326a_psecaa_lookup` from the engineering programme-management system (Siemens Teamcenter, PTC Windchill, Dassault ENOVIA) and from the certification team's GRC tool (ServiceNow GRC, RSA Archer). (2) The Chief Engineer or designated security focal updates the milestone_completed_at and authority_acceptance_letter_ref fields when the FAA ACO or EASA PCM issues acceptance. (3) Schedule UC-22.60.1 daily at 06:00 programme-local; route `risk_score >= 80` to the Chief Engineer + CISO + Programme Director at severity P1, `risk_score >= 60` at severity P2. (4) Build the `DO-326A PSecAA Milestone Health` dashboard with per-programme, per-authority, and per-airframe-type views.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.60.1` archived daily to `audit_evidence` with `do326a_clause="DO-326A-s2-2"`. Every row pins (project_id, applicant, type_certificate_or_stc, airframe_type, psecaa_version, psecaa_acceptance_status, certification_authority, milestone_due_date, milestone_status, days_to_due, risk_score).

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Allow a project's PSecAA acceptance milestone to slip past its due date without an updated PSecAA submission. UC-22.60.1 MUST flag within 24 hours with risk_score >= 80.

### Negative scenario

Submit and obtain certification-authority PSecAA acceptance 30 days before the milestone. UC-22.60.1 MUST record `milestone_status=accepted` and `risk_score=0`.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- Splunk Enterprise >= 9.2 or Splunk Cloud.
- KV Store `do326a_psecaa_lookup` (created via `collections.conf` and seeded from the programme-management system).
- ServiceNow GRC project register or equivalent.
- A documented mapping from internal milestone IDs to DO-326A artefact deliverables.

## Step 1 - PSecAA KV Store

```ini
[do326a_psecaa_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.project_id = string
field.applicant = string
field.type_certificate_or_stc = string
field.airframe_type = string
field.psecaa_version = string
field.psecaa_acceptance_status = string
field.certification_authority = string
field.milestone_id = string
field.milestone_due_date = time
field.milestone_completed_at = time
field.authority_acceptance_letter_ref = string
field.project_status = string
```

Seed from the programme-management system on a daily schedule (modular input or `outputlookup`).

## Step 2 - Milestone catalogue

Maintain a milestone catalogue mapping internal IDs to DO-326A artefacts: PSecAA acceptance, CSI register baseline, Threat Conditions list freeze, Security Risk Assessment completion, Security Architecture freeze, Security Effectiveness Demonstration, CASI delivery to operator.

## Step 3 - Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.60.1: DO-326A PSecAA Milestone Health]
cron_schedule = 0 6 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -1d@d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.60.1,reg=DO-326A,clause=DO-326A-s2-2
alert.severity = 5
```

## Step 4 - Runbooks

**`risk_score >= 80`** (owner: Chief Engineer + CISO, severity P1): Within 24 hours, schedule a programme-status call with the FAA ACO / EASA PCM; within 7 days, deliver a written remediation plan with an acceptable new milestone date.

**`risk_score 40-79`** (owner: Security Focal, severity P2): Within 5 business days, drive milestone closure.

## Step 5 - Aviation-specific notes

- **Joint TC validation**: a project may have parallel FAA / EASA / TCCA / CAA review; ensure each authority's milestone clock is tracked independently.
- **Major modification vs Type Certificate**: STCs have a lighter PSecAA but still require acceptance; do not exclude them from monitoring.
- **Issue Papers / CRIs**: legacy projects may have project-specific Special Conditions (Issue Papers in the US, Certification Review Items in EASA); include their milestones in the catalogue.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup do326a_psecaa_lookup
| eval now_ts = now()
| eval days_to_due = round((milestone_due_date - now_ts) / 86400, 1)
| eval days_overdue = case(isnull(milestone_completed_at) AND now_ts > milestone_due_date, round((now_ts - milestone_due_date) / 86400, 1), 1=1, 0)
| eval milestone_status = case(
    isnotnull(milestone_completed_at), "complete",
    isnotnull(authority_acceptance_letter_ref), "accepted",
    isnull(milestone_completed_at) AND now_ts > milestone_due_date, "overdue",
    days_to_due <= 14, "at-risk",
    1=1, "in-window")
| eval risk_score = case(
    milestone_status = "overdue" AND days_overdue > 30, 100,
    milestone_status = "overdue" AND days_overdue > 7, 90,
    milestone_status = "overdue", 80,
    milestone_status = "at-risk" AND psecaa_acceptance_status != "accepted", 70,
    milestone_status = "at-risk", 40,
    1=1, 0)
| where risk_score > 0
| sort 0 -risk_score, -days_overdue, +days_to_due
| table project_id applicant type_certificate_or_stc airframe_type psecaa_version psecaa_acceptance_status certification_authority milestone_id milestone_due_date days_to_due days_overdue milestone_status risk_score
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) Active projects by PSecAA acceptance status (accepted / submitted / draft / overdue) - stacked bar by certification authority. (Panel 2) Milestone heat map (project x days-to-due). (Panel 3) Open critical-risk projects sorted by risk_score. (Panel 4) 12-month milestone-on-time performance trend.

## Known False Positives

1. **Authority-approved milestone deferral**: the FAA ACO / EASA PCM may agree to a milestone deferral (typically via a written re-baseline of the project schedule); ensure the new due date is loaded into the KV Store with the authority concurrence reference. 2. **Programme on hold**: a project that is paused pending business decision (programme parking) should be marked with `project_status=on-hold` and excluded from the alert. 3. **PSecAA Revision in flight**: a PSecAA Revision being prepared in response to scope change is briefly in `draft` status; allow a 30-day grace from the trigger event.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `do326a_22_60_1_exceptions` with the Chief Engineer + CISO documented approval, an explicit expiry date, and a written justification.

## References

- [RTCA DO-326A - Airworthiness Security Process Specification](https://www.rtca.org/standards/list-of-available-documents/)
- [FAA AC 20-186 - Airworthiness and Operational Approval for Aircraft Network Security Program (ANSP)](https://www.faa.gov/regulations_policies/advisory_circulars/index.cfm/go/document.information/documentid/1039753)
- [EASA AMC 20-42 - Airworthiness Information Security Risk Assessment](https://www.easa.europa.eu/en/document-library/acceptable-means-of-compliance-and-guidance-materials)
